steps.title: Use Wong's examples
steps.content: Wong says that during distractions and daydreaming, a point, phrase, title, image, connection, or epiphany can emerge. Even before words appear, ideas for writing are forming.

steps.title: Match writing-process work
steps.content: Choice A says she is deceiving herself, which the text does not suggest. Choice C focuses on gathering sources rather than forming ideas. Choice D says she is being soothed by others' works, but the passage emphasizes generating material. The meaning is part of writing. The answer is B.
